briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 5, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Domestic Climate Crisis Warning and Deepening Ecosystem Changes
sections.content: According to recent analyses, severe concerns are rising as abnormal climate disasters such as wildfires, heatwaves, droughts, and torrential rains are predicted to occur simultaneously in Korea in 2025 [11]. Seasonal changes are already clearly detectable, with spring festival timings becoming erratic due to climate change [1, 10]. In particular, Green Korea United warned that the ecosystem crisis is accelerating, reporting a rapid spread of mass die-offs of coniferous trees in the Baekdudaegan mountain range and national park areas, with Cheonwangbong-Jungbong Peak in Jirisan Mountain transforming into a vast area of dead trees, and Korean fir trees experiencing up to a 90% die-off [9, 56]. These phenomena clearly demonstrate that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future concern.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Domestic Policies and Community Efforts for Carbon Neutrality
sections.content: On Arbor Day, both ruling and opposition parties emphasized the importance of carbon neutrality and climate change response, pledging to pursue responsible policies [3, 14, 16]. Various local governments and organizations, including Chungcheongnam-do, Gangwon Technopark, Ulsan Coast Guard, and Gwangjin-gu, led the way in practicing carbon neutrality by holding tree-planting events with residents and employees [13, 17, 22, 28, 31, 32]. Notably, Gyeongsangnam-do, Pohang-si, and Gumi-si are incentivizing voluntary carbon reduction by offering up to 100,000 won to citizens who reduce their driving distance through the automobile carbon neutrality point system [15, 18, 19, 20, 21, 23, 26, 27, 34, 35, 36]. Meanwhile, discussions on the direction of amendments to the Carbon Neutrality Framework Act are actively underway, raising expectations for strengthening the institutional foundation [12, 38].

sections.title: 🌱 Acceleration of Renewable Energy Transition and Climate Technology Innovation Trends
sections.content: The Seoul Metropolitan Government is actively supporting technology-based climate action, including recruiting companies for innovative climate technology demonstration support, offering up to 200 million won and demonstration sites [2, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]. The promotion of renewable energy projects is active both domestically and internationally; Big Green Power plans to pursue an additional $5 billion renewable energy project in Korea, and LX Pantos and SK Innovation E&S have begun a collaboration to utilize logistics centers as renewable energy power plants [39, 47, 51]. Furthermore, Chuncheon City plans to achieve energy independence by installing floating solar power on Soyanggang Dam and Chuncheon Dam [53], and India is accelerating its energy independence by constructing the world's largest battery storage facility in a massive solar park five times the size of Paris [48]. Efforts in technological innovation continue to address the inherent intermittency problem of renewable energy, with EnerVenue attracting $300 million in investment with NASA technology [45].

sections.title: 🌍 International Energy Market Volatility and the Importance of Climate Change Education
sections.content: Energy crises originating from the Middle East and regional variables are shaking Southeast Asia's power generation strategies, once again emphasizing the urgency of demand management and renewable energy transition [49, 52]. Meanwhile, concerns have been raised about the impact of federal funding cuts on climate change initiatives, highlighting the importance of securing stable funding for climate action [58]. Internationally, efforts to educate and raise awareness about climate change continue, with the Nebraska State Museum hosting monthly science labs on climate change and students envisioning the future [59, 60]. Domestically, Daejeon's Daedeok-gu is contributing to raising environmental awareness among future generations by operating a carbon neutrality experience program for children [29], and Hyundai Motor is expanding its electric vehicle car-sharing service in rural Spain, supporting eco-friendly mobility [54].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, the climate crisis is having a profound impact on all aspects of our lives. However, at the same time, we have also observed various policies and innovative efforts actively underway both domestically and internationally to overcome this challenge. This is a time when sustained interest and action become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: 참가자 1000명, 쓰레기는 1개인 마라톤 [현장 화보]
articles.description: Participants are seen crossing the finish line at Peace Park in Mapo-gu, Seoul, on April 5th, during the '2026 Muhaerun,' a zero-waste eco-friendly marathon. On Arbor Day, over a thousand participants ran 10km without generating new waste. This year marks the second iteration of the '2026 Muhaerun,' focusing on waste reduction.

summary.phenomenon: On April 5th, an eco-friendly marathon called '2026 Muhaerun' was held at Peace Park in Mapo-gu, Seoul, where over a thousand participants ran 10km without generating new waste.

articles.original_description: 쓰레기 없는 친환경 마라톤인 ‘2026 무해런’이 열린 5일 서울 마포구 평화의 공원에서 참가자들이 결승지점으로 들어오고 있다. 정효진 기자식목일인 5일 지구를 생각하는 마라톤이 열렸다. 이날 이른 아침부터 서울 마포구 평화의 공원에 모인 천여 명의 참가자들은 ‘새로운 쓰레기를 만들지 않고’ 10km를 뛰었다.올해가 2회차인 ‘2026 무해런’은 쓰레기 없···

articles.title: Conifers in Baekdudaegan Dying Off… Up to 90% of Korean Fir Trees Dead
articles.original_title: 백두대간 침엽수 말라 죽는다···구상나무 최대 90% 고사
articles.description: Jirisan Cheonwangbong-Jungbong, a major conifer habitat in the Baekdudaegan subalpine zone, has become a vast area of dead trees. A report by an environmental organization, Green Korea United, reveals a rapid spread of mass conifer die-offs across the Baekdudaegan mountain range and national parks. The report, '2026 Baekdudaegan National Park Climate Crisis Conifer Report,' published on April 5th, suggests some species are nearing extinction due to climate change.

summary.phenomenon: A report by Green Korea United, published on April 5th, indicates a rapid spread of mass conifer die-offs across the Baekdudaegan mountain range and national parks. Notably, Jirisan Cheonwangbong-Jungbong has transformed into a vast area of dead trees, with up to 90% of Korean fir trees (Gusangnamu) having died.
summary.cause: These conifer die-offs are attributed to the impacts of climate change.
summary.outlook: Analysis suggests that some tree species have entered the extinction phase.

articles.original_description: 백두대간 아고산대 최대 침엽수 서식지인 지리산 천왕봉중봉이 거대한 고사목지대로 변해 있다. 녹색연합 제공백두대간과 국립공원 일대에서 침엽수 집단 고사가 빠르게 확산되고 있다는 환경단체 보고서가 나왔다. 기후변화 영향으로 일부 수종은 멸종 단계에 접어들었다는 분석이다.녹색연합이 5일 발간한 ‘2026 백두대간 국립공원 기후위기 침엽수 보고서’를 보면, 201···

articles.original_title: 거북이도 OK, 쓰레기·교통 통제 없이 ‘무해하게 달린다’···유일한 문제는 내년이 불투명한 것
articles.description: Participants are seen starting at Peace Park in Mapo-gu, Seoul, on April 5th, for the 2nd eco-friendly marathon '2026 Muhaerun.' The event, which included 'Rabbit' and 'Turtle' teams, fostered a relaxed atmosphere for recording times. Reusable cups, paper bibs, and recycled timing chips were provided, with participants expressing appreciation for the initiative. However, the event's reliance on sponsorship due to a lack of profit raises concerns about its long-term sustainability.

summary.phenomenon: On April 5th, the second eco-friendly marathon, '2026 Muhaerun,' was held at Peace Park in Mapo-gu, Seoul. Participants ran without generating waste, utilizing reusable cups, paper bibs, and recycled timing chips.
summary.outlook: As this event relies on sponsorship due to a lack of profit, its future beyond this year is uncertain, raising concerns about its long-term sustainability.
